texto: Trying to corral the suburban stampede with a bunch of school buses was like herding cats. Actually, it was worse than herding cats. It was herding white people, earth's only species with a greater sense of entitlement than a cat.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tanner Colby: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a educacao. O trecho “Trying to corral the suburban stampede with a bunch of school buses was like herding cats. Actual…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, cats, humor pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de humor e real-estate.

texto: In the twenty-first century, the visions of J.C. Nichols and Walt Disney have come full circle and joined. “Neighborhoods” are increasingly “developments,” corporate theme parks. But corporations aren’t interested in the messy ebb and flow of humanity. They want stability and predictable rates of return. And although racial discrimination is no longer a stated policy for real estate brokers and developers, racial and social homogeneity are still firmly embedded in America’s collective idea of stability; that’s what our new landlords are thinking even if they are not saying it. (138)
explicacao: Orientação direta de Tanner Colby sobre temas ligados a amizade. A afirmação “In the twenty-first century, the visions of J.C. Nichols and Walt Disney have come full circle an…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amizade, community, cross-racial, útil em momentos de termino e cross-racial.

texto: Vann also came to realize something else. As offensive as the lack of cultural awareness in the office was, part of that deficit was his own. They didn’t understand him, but he didn’t understand them, either. (197)

texto: Many people in Nixon’s camp had genuine faith in affirmative action. It wasn’t designed to fail, but it wasn’t designed to succeed, either; the intent behind it was not rooted in a desire to help black people attain equal standing in society. It was riot insurance. It was a financial incentive for blacks to stay in their own communities and out of the suburbs. (183)
explicacao: Citação de Tanner Colby sobre temas ligados a fe. “Many people in Nixon’s camp had genuine faith in affirmative action. It wasn’t designed to fail, …” articula fe, affirmative-action, community em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como community e diversity.
